cases.resolution: Courtesy refund posted by Maestro after policy check.
cases.short_summary: Customer requests reversal of a single overdraft fee after deposit timing issue.
cases.requested_action: Apply courtesy credit when eligible; otherwise explain policy and alternatives.
cases.ai_summary: The customer is asking for a one-time overdraft fee reversal. History and amount are within frontline courtesy policy.
cases.policy_recommendation: Auto-approve because the refund is under the threshold and there is no recent courtesy refund recorded in the sandbox history.
cases.customer_message: We reviewed your account and reversed the overdraft fee as a one-time courtesy.

cases.resolution: Pending supervisor approval for refund exception.
cases.short_summary: Customer requests reversal of multiple fees and mentions recent hardship.
cases.requested_action: Review hardship context, prior courtesy history, and approve or reject exception.
cases.ai_summary: The customer is requesting a larger courtesy refund and cites hardship. The amount requires supervisor review.
cases.policy_recommendation: Route to Action Center for supervisor approval with account history and policy evidence attached.

cases.resolution: Escalated due to repeated complaint language and regulatory mention.
cases.short_summary: Customer disputes repeated fees and threatens to file a complaint.
cases.requested_action: Acknowledge complaint, avoid promising outcome, and create escalation record.
cases.ai_summary: The customer is disputing fees and using complaint language that requires a controlled escalation path.
cases.policy_recommendation: Do not auto-refund. Create an executive resolution case and preserve the audit trail.
